evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Build automated checks for freshness, completeness, consistency and accuracy; establish testing standards; maintained documentation
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour-focused: 25% of role is testing/documentation ownership with explicit standards for schema, business rules, and metric validation.
evidence.domain_risk: UK media/publishing platform; moderate domain risk.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Moderate: media company without explicit financial/regulatory language, though works with large diverse datasets.
evidence.collaboration_width: Data Engineering; Analytics teams; Product; Data Science; commercial stakeholders
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools in responsibilities (dbt, Snowflake, Airflow), dedicated data team with multiple specialties, growing analytics function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design, build and maintain scalable, reusable, well-documented data models; develop reusable semantic layers, metrics and core entities
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Hiring manager: precise methodology (semantic layers, core entities, curated datasets) and clear application context (BI, product, data science).
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Focus on building shared metrics and trusted data products for internal BI, Data Science, and Analytics teams—not external commercial.
evidence.autonomy_level: take end-to-end ownership of key data products; Build deep expertise in one or more data domains
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Strategic: end-to-end product ownership and domain expertise development, not just executing on specs.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools or AI-infrastructure expected from the candidate.
evidence.testing_framing: Build automated checks for freshness, completeness, consistency and accuracy; establish testing standards including schema, business rule and metric validation
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Responsibility: testing and validation standards ownership (25% of role) as core accountability.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: trusted, reusable data products; clear, consistent definitions and metrics; Build automated checks for accuracy
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High loss aversion: trustworthiness, consistency, and accuracy are primary—preventing bad data reaching BI/Product/DS teams.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Transform complex raw and intermediate data into curated datasets; identify where analytics engineering can improve
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Fix/scale: transforming existing raw data structures into curated products, improving quality and consistency.
